message:
fix(quic): packet number exhaustion guard and RTT measurement correctness

Connection (connection.rs):
  next_packet_number() now returns Result instead of a bare u64. When
  the packet number reaches 2^62 - 1 (the QUIC protocol limit per
  RFC 9000 Section 17.1), it returns an InvalidState error instead of
  silently wrapping via saturating_add. The previous behavior would
  reuse packet numbers, causing catastrophic protocol violations
  (duplicate packet numbers break loss detection, ACK processing, and
  key phase tracking). All callers now propagate the error.

Transport/LossRecovery (transport.rs):
  RTT measurement fix: the previous code updated RTT from every
  ack-eliciting packet in the ACK range, but RFC 9002 Section 5.1
  specifies that RTT samples must only come from the largest newly
  acknowledged packet. The fix tracks largest_newly_acked_pn and its
  send time, then updates RTT once after processing all ACKs.

  Additionally, ack_delay is now only applied for ApplicationData
  space packets (Initial and Handshake ACKs have no peer-introduced
  delay per RFC 9002 Section 5.3).

  New method: LossRecovery::discard_space() removes all tracked
  packets for a given packet number space and adjusts bytes_in_flight.
  This is needed when discarding Initial/Handshake keys after the
  handshake completes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
